subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: A man purchases a life insurance policy on his 40th birthday. The policy will pay 5000 only if he dies before his 50th birthday and will pay 0 otherwise. The length of lifetime, in years, of a male born the same year as the insured has the cumulative distribution function $F(t) = \begin{cases} 1 - e^{\frac{1 - 1.1^t}{1000}} & t > 0 \\ 0 & t \le 0 \end{cases}$ Calculate the expected payment to the man under this policy.
